title: Optimized polyepitope neoantigen DNA vaccines elicit neoantigen-specific immune responses in preclinical models and in clinical translation
indication_summary: 2 preclinical murine breast-cancer models (E0771, 4T1.2) + 1 human pancreatic NET (compassionate use)

patients.quoted_text: E0771 / C57BL/6 (H-2b) polyepitope neoantigen DNA vaccine; 3 immunogenic

preclinical_efficacy.note: vaccine alone had only marginal impact in prophylactic or therapeutic settings (data not shown); did not protect from transplanted tumour
E0771 is anti-PD-L1 responsive; neoantigen-specific T cells persisted to day 26 only in the combination arm (TIL dextramer P=0.0381, ANOVA)

patients.quoted_text: 4T1.2 / Balb/c (H-2d) polyepitope neoantigen DNA vaccine; 4 immunogenic

preclinical_efficacy.note: 4T1.2 is resistant to anti-PD-L1 monotherapy (Fig S3a); vaccine+ICB combination not yet tested ('further investigation needed')

patients.quoted_text: 25yo male, metastatic pancreatic NET, compassionate-use pGTB16 DNA vaccine; 3/13 neoantigens immunogenic by IFN-g ELISpot
patients.indication: metastatic pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor (Lynch syndrome, MSI-high)

evidence.assay_detail: CD8 response; IFN-g ELISpot; MT and WT bind H-2Db equally, WT cross-reactivity at high peptide conc (Fig S4)
IFN-g ELISpot after 12-day in vitro stim; restim with individual long peptide; above pre-vaccine background
